---
title: "R Vectors Notes"
output: html_document
---

# VECTORS

-Is a 1-D collection of elements that are of the same type.


-Are created using the 'c()' concatenation.

    numbers <- c(23, 45, 89)

-Creating a vector with a numerical values in a sequence:

    numbers <- 1:10
    <!-- output is the numbers from 1-10 -->

-To find the length of a vector we use 'length()'

    fruits <- c("banana", "apple", "orange")

-To sort items in a vector alphabetically or numerically use: 'sort()'

    grades <- c("A", "B", "F", "E", "C", "D")
    <!-- sort the items alphabetically -->

-To access items in a vector, refer to its index number inside []. The first item having index 1 and so on.

    names <- c("Jack", "Jill", "Jane", "John")

    names[2]
    <!-- access the second item, Jill -->

    names[c(2, 3)]
    <!-- access multiple elements by referring to different index positions using the 'c()' function -->

    names[c(-1)]
    <!-- access all the items apart from the first one -->

    names[4] <- "James"
    <!-- change the value of a specific item -->

## Logical Indexing 101.

-Imagine there are a row of lockers, and each locker has a number inside.

-There is a list with 'Open' or 'Closed' for each locker.

-Logical indexing is 'Give me the numbers from only the lockers saying "Open" '

-In R: the data vector, is the row of lockers, TRUE/FALSE list being our signs and the '[]' being the action of opening the selected lockers.

-Syntax:

    data[condition]
    <!-- condition is something giving TRUE or FALSE for every element -->
